Why the US is facing strategic defeat

The United States is attempting to sustain deterrence in multiple theaters while its industrial base remains tuned for short wars and limited campaigns.

Taiwan, missile defense, and regional stockpiles all compete for the same constrained pool of production. THAAD batteries and interceptors are a concrete example of this tension.

Strategy is not just a story about resolve. It is also an argument about throughput.

Theater math

A serious contingency in East Asia would demand munition flows that are difficult to improvise after the shooting starts.

  • Production capacity matters.
  • Logistics depth matters.
